checkedConditions: CommTaskControl accepts an optional request_nonce propagated from swarm operation_id for explicit retry idempotency.
When wake/start/resume task_control is called with request_nonce, duplicate calls replay the original TaskControl response instead of repeating side effects.
Task control behavior without operation_id remains unchanged so intentional repeated control calls still execute normally.
Existing retry/reassign/replace/salvage assign_task-backed control paths remain compatible with mutation replay.
Focused protocol/server/tool tests, cargo fmt/check, git diff --check, selfdev build/reload, governance, commit, and push pass.
evidence: crates/jcode-protocol/src/lib.rs adds optional request_nonce to CommTaskControl and protocol roundtrip coverage validates serialization.
src/tool/communicate.rs maps explicit task_control operation_id to an op-prefixed request_nonce; calls without operation_id continue sending None.

src/server/comm_control.rs wraps task_control in an outer mutation keyed by coordinator, swarm_id, action, and request_nonce, capturing and persisting final TaskControl/AssignTask/Error responses only after side effects finish.

src/server/comm_control_tests/task_control.rs proves duplicate wake with the same request_nonce replays the original response and leaves exactly one queued soft interrupt containing the original instructions.
Focused verification task 245919s0gj and selfdev build task 315745xdd2 passed; reload succeeded. Initial task 23259653xr was only a one-second wrapper timeout and was recorded separately.
notes: The idempotency key is explicit-only: default repeated task_control calls remain non-idempotent, while retry/reassign/replace/salvage can replay captured assign_task-backed responses under the outer task_control nonce.
